observations.title: state フィールド名が未明示
observations.rationale: 要件 3 に「検出した path と変更種別を state に記録する」とあるが、JobState に追加する具体的なフィールド名は指定されていない。schema.ts（persisted-format surface）への変更が伴うため、design step でフィールド名を確定し ADR に残すと追跡性が上がる。

observations.title: resolvePipelineForbiddenSurfaces のバイパスが必要
observations.rationale: src/config/schema.ts の resolvePipelineForbiddenSurfaces は pipelineId === 'fast' のときのみ surfaces を返す。本変更では全 pipeline で pipeline.fast.forbiddenSurfaces を参照するため、実装者はこの helper をバイパスするか新アクセサを追加する必要がある。blocking ではないが実装時の注意点として記録する。

toolResult.reason: Wrote design.md, spec.md, and tasks.md under specrunner/changes/main-checkout-write-detection/ on branch change/main-checkout-write-detection-18ba00c4. Plan adds a before/after main-checkout guard-path snapshot at agent-step boundaries via a new RuntimeStrategy seam (snapshotMainCheckoutGuard), pure diff/resolve helpers in src/core/step/main-checkout-guard.ts, escalation to awaiting-resume mirroring the timeout path, and a JobState mainCheckoutDrift record. No src/ edits performed (planning only).

findings.title: kind: "modified" for before-only entries is semantically imprecise
findings.rationale: When a file appears in before-snapshot but not in after-snapshot (was dirty before step, clean after), T-02 assigns kind: "modified". This actually means the agent committed or reverted a file on main checkout — potentially more suspicious than a plain content change. Users reading the CLI drift output may be confused by the label.

findings.title: mainCheckoutDrift field lifecycle after successful resume is unspecified
findings.rationale: The spec says to record drift in state but does not say whether mainCheckoutDrift is cleared after the user resolves the issue and the step succeeds on resume. The natural interpretation is to keep it as an audit trail, but leaving this implicit may cause implementation inconsistency.

findings.title: DSM closure Open Question left for implementation-time verification without a fallback plan
findings.rationale: The design's Open Question about verifying no new inter-layer reverse edges are introduced is deferred to implementation time. If a violation is found, the task says to escalate back to spec-review. Adding explicit acceptance criteria for the import graph check in T-02 would reduce the chance of late-stage hand-back.

message: spec-review iteration 1 completed with verdict: approved
